FQA-001.issue: PHP manually extracted attributes from $attributes array
FQA-001.verification: Confirmed lines 10-27 removed. No `$attributes['key']` patterns found in entire file. All attribute variables now use auto-extracted values from render_callback's extract($attributes, EXTR_SKIP).
FQA-001.frontendVerification: All 10 test page blocks render correctly with auto-extracted variables. Verified: dark mode classes apply, heading sizes toggle (h2/h3), column reversing works, row reversing works, eyebrow text displays, custom button labels render.

FQA-002.issue: Missing @var docblock for auto-extracted camelCase attribute variables
FQA-002.verification: Confirmed @var docblock added (lines 5-17). All camelCase attribute variables documented: $selectedPost, $isReversed, $reverseRows, $darkMode, $buttonLabel, $headingSize, $eyebrow. Also documents $block and $content. Format matches theme convention.
FQA-002.lintImpact: PHP_CodeSniffer will no longer warn about camelCase variables. Docblock documents that these variables come from block attributes (which use JavaScript camelCase convention) rather than being local PHP variables that should follow WordPress snake_case convention.

FQA-003.verification: Confirmed line 70 removed. TSX now goes directly from firstCategory declaration (line 69) to categoryAccentColor hook (line 72). No unused variable error in lint output.

buildVerification.result: Build completed successfully with only expected asset size warnings. No TypeScript errors.

issueTypes.acceptability: Acceptable - standard recommended control for this UI pattern

attributesFunctional.evidence: All 10 blocks display correct post content (title, excerpt, featured image, category)
Blocks 1, 3, 8 have reversed column layout (content on left, media on right)
Blocks 2, 3, 8, 9 have dark mode classes applied correctly
Block 4 uses h3, block 8 uses h3, others use h2 as expected
Custom button labels render: 'Read Full Story', 'Explore This Story', 'View Full Article on Our Blog', 'Read the Full Story'
Blocks 6, 9 have reverseRows attribute which affects mobile ordering
Blocks 5, 8, 9 display eyebrow text next to topic tag

regressionChecks: All images display with correct alt text
All CTA links render with aria-label attributes
Dark mode pseudo-element backgrounds apply correctly
Heading hierarchy correct (h2 for regular, h3 for small)
Topic tags render with category names

newIssuesCheck.details: No new issues introduced by fixes. All auto-extracted variables work correctly in conditionals, class_name() calls, and output contexts.

beforeFixes.description: Manual attribute extraction violated theme convention, missing docblock caused lint warnings, unused variable caused lint error

afterFixes.description: Code now follows theme convention for auto-extracted attributes, docblock documents variable origins, no unused variables

codeQualityImprovement.maintainabilityGain: Significant - removed 18 lines of redundant code, documented auto-extraction pattern, aligned with theme standards

recommendations: Block is now production-ready from functional QA perspective
Code quality improved - follows theme conventions
Frontend rendering verified - all attributes functional
Experimental component warnings are acceptable for now
